channels.title: Rhythms of China
channels.description: Acclaimed musician Bei Bei Monter leads us on an historical and musical exploration of the guzheng, a stringed instrument that dates back almost 2,500 years to Ancient China.
channels.about: Bei Bei Monter is one of the world’s premier guzheng musicians. Born in Chengdu, China, Bei Bei began playing at age seven. She studied at the Hong Kong Academy for Performing Arts, and collaborates with musicians across funk, jazz, pop, electronica, hip-hop, and dance.

channels.title: Rhythms of India
channels.description: Renowned sitarist Aloke Dasgupta, an expert in the Hindustani and Carnactic traditions of Indian classical music, guides us on a voyage into the rich and complex musical world of the Indian subcontinent.
channels.about: Aloke Dasgupta is among the most outstanding and original sitar players in the North Indian classical style. He is known for collaborating across genres, while keeping the integrity of classical-style sitar intact. Aloke has recorded and performed with Ozzy Osbourne, Mick Jagger, and Gwen Stefani and become the go-to sitar performer in artistic projects.

channels.title: Rhythms of Puerto Rico
channels.description: Join Emmy-nominated composer, Fabiola Mendez on a musical journey to her homeland of Puerto Rico to explore the history and culture of ‘musica jibara’ and the cuatro, the island’s traditional guitar-like instrument.
channels.about: Fabiola Méndez is a Puerto Rican cuatrista, singer, Emmy-nominated composer, and educator. She was the first graduate of Berklee College of Music to play the Puerto Rican cuatro as principal instrument and was awarded the "Latin Artist of the Year 2023" by the Boston Music Awards.

channels.title: Sleepy Time Sports
channels.description: Long-time pals, Jeremiah and Joe chat about sports culture – from team logos and celebrating too early, to the history of the Heisman trophy.
channels.about:
Nominated as Best of the Fest" at the 10,000 Laughs Comedy Festival, Jeremiah Coughlan (he/him) is an outgoing stand-up comic with an energetic, self-deprecating style.

Joe Simons (he/him) is an experienced broadcaster who’s covered the Pacific Northwest for 15 years. He won awards for his role as co-anchor of Portland's Afternoon News on KXL Radio, and co-hosted Blazers Outsiders, a Portland Trail Blazers postgame TV show for NBC Sports Northwest."

channels.title: Rhythms of The Gambia
channels.description: Master kora player Salieu Suso takes you on a sonic tour of his homeland in West Africa’s Republic of The Gambia, where we explore rich musical traditions and personal stories as you drift off for the night.
channels.about: Born into a family of traditional African jalis (musician-historians), Salieu Suso began training on the 21-stringed kora at the age of eight, and began his professional career by the age of 15. He has performed widely throughout the U.S., Africa and Europe, and is known for his diverse range of musical collaborations.

channels.title: Rhythms of Iran
channels.description: Join esteemed multi-instrumentalist Hamed Habibpour as we dive into the traditional music and sounds of his homeland of Iran with the santur, the saz, and the setar.
channels.about: Hamed Habibpour is a multi-instrumentalist who has performed in festivals across Iran, Turkey, and Tunisia. With a passion for music rooted in his childhood, Hamed began learning the santur from his father at the age of six. Hamed studied Iranian traditional music at the University of Art and was mentored by esteemed masters at the University of Tehran.
